Details: Ozone is effective for the removal of metolachlor degradates in drinking water applications. The removal efficiencies were reported in the range of 63 to 100 percent in bench-scale studies and 50 (metolachlor OA) to greater than 57 percent (metolachlor ESA) in full-scale monitoring. Full-scale studies and were affected by the water quality parameters, ozone dose and contact time. Ozonation byproducts were not identified in the studies.
